groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Topic, goal, audience, facts, language, sources, type, length, takeaway.

groups.title: Content & Structure
groups.description: Opening, depth, perspective, numbering, and ending style.

groups.title: Brand & Conversion
groups.description: Tone, voice, CTA, destination link, and commercial disclosure.

groups.title: Compliance & Constraints
groups.description: Sensitive topics, claim restrictions, and additional context.

variables.label: Topic and angle
variables.placeholder: Explain what the thread is about and the specific angle you want to take.
variables.hint: Be specific enough to distinguish this thread from a general post about the topic.

help.why: The model cannot safely choose the user’s intended subject and point of view.
help.example: Why short product posts convert better than feature dumps
help.avoid: A generic topic with no angle.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main outcome
variables.hint: Choose the single most important result the thread should achieve.

help.why: The goal determines the thread’s progression, emphasis, ending, and CTA.
help.avoid: Stacking sell + discuss + authority as equal goals.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Who should read this, and what do they already know or care about?
variables.hint: Describe the intended readers, their context, and their level of familiarity.

help.why: Audience knowledge and motivation materially affect terminology, examples, and depth.
help.example: Who should read this, and what do they already know or care about?
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Key points and facts
variables.placeholder: List the facts, arguments, steps, examples, or details the thread must use.
variables.hint: Include only information you want treated as factual or essential.

help.why: The thread requires user-supplied substance and must not invent supporting facts.
help.example: Only verified steps, numbers, and examples
help.avoid: Invented stats, quotes, or personal experiences.

variables.label: Output language
variables.placeholder: For example: English, Ukrainian, Spanish
variables.hint: You may also specify a regional variant, such as British English.

help.why: Language cannot always be inferred from the topic or source material.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Source material
variables.placeholder: Paste notes, research, quotes, links, product details, or an existing draft.
variables.hint: The model may reorganize supplied material but must not invent missing evidence.

help.why: Source material improves accuracy when the thread depends on research, quotations, or product information.
help.example: Paste notes, research, quotes, links, product details, or an existing draft.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Thread type
variables.placeholder: Select a thread format
variables.hint: Choose the format closest to the way the idea should unfold.

help.why: Different X threads require different narrative structures rather than a generic hook-body-CTA formula.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Thread length
variables.placeholder: Select a target length
variables.hint: The final count may be reduced when the idea does not justify the selected length.

help.why: The desired thread length affects pacing and the amount of detail allocated to each point.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Main takeaway
variables.placeholder: What should readers remember after finishing the thread?
variables.hint: Leave blank when the takeaway can be inferred from the key points.

help.why: A supplied takeaway helps prevent the thread from becoming a disconnected list of posts.
help.example: What should readers remember after finishing the thread?
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Opening style
variables.placeholder: Choose how the first post opens
variables.hint: Auto selects the strongest truthful opening for the topic and goal.

help.why: The opening approach changes how the thread earns attention without relying on clickbait.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Level of detail
variables.placeholder: Select the desired depth
variables.hint: This controls information density, not the number of posts by itself.

help.why: Users may need either a fast overview or a more substantial explanation within the same post count.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Writing perspective
variables.placeholder: Select who appears to be speaking
variables.hint: Do not use first-person experience unless it is supported by the supplied information.

help.why: Perspective determines whether the thread reads as personal experience, brand communication, or independent explanation.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Post numbering
variables.placeholder: Choose how posts are numbered
variables.hint: Numbering is included inside the character allowance when used.

help.why: Numbering affects readability, character usage, and the appearance of the published thread.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Ending style
variables.placeholder: Choose how the thread finishes
variables.hint: The ending should resolve the thread rather than abruptly adding a sales pitch.

help.why: The intended ending changes how the final posts consolidate or convert reader attention.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Select the overall tone
variables.hint: The tone will remain appropriate to the subject and audience.

help.why: Tone is a genuine brand and communication preference that cannot always be inferred.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Voice guidance
variables.placeholder: Describe your usual voice or paste a short example of how you write.
variables.hint: Mention distinctive preferences, not general rules such as “write naturally.”

help.why: Specific voice guidance helps the output sound consistent with the user or brand.
help.example: Describe your usual voice or paste a short example of how you write.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Desired reader action
variables.placeholder: Select the main action
variables.hint: Auto chooses a proportionate CTA or omits it when none is useful.

help.why: The desired action determines whether and how the final post should convert attention.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: CTA details
variables.placeholder: Describe the action, offer, resource, or next step readers should take.
variables.hint: Include only one primary next step and any information needed to explain it.

help.why: The model cannot safely invent an offer, resource, booking step, or conversion promise.
help.example: Reply with your biggest posting bottleneck
help.avoid: Multiple competing CTAs in the final posts.

variables.label: Destination URL
variables.placeholder: https://example.com/resource
variables.hint: Only supplied URLs may appear in the generated thread.

help.why: A link-based CTA requires an accurate user-supplied destination.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Commercial relationship
variables.placeholder: Select any relevant relationship
variables.hint: This helps the thread avoid presenting promotion as independent opinion.

help.why: Commercial affiliation may materially change wording and disclosure requirements.
help.avoid: Presenting owned products as independent reviews.

variables.label: Disclosure wording
variables.placeholder: For example: I built this product, or Sponsored by…
variables.hint: Leave blank to generate a brief neutral disclosure based on the selected relationship.

help.why: The user may need approved or legally required disclosure wording.
help.example: For example: I built this product, or Sponsored by…
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Sensitive topic
variables.placeholder: Select when additional caution is needed
variables.hint: This enables cautious wording and verification notes where appropriate.

help.why: High-stakes subjects require different claim handling and may depend on jurisdiction or current authoritative information.
help.avoid: Medical/legal claims without approved wording.

variables.label: Claims and restrictions
variables.placeholder: List prohibited claims, required wording, jurisdiction, confidentiality limits, or topics to avoid.
variables.hint: Use this for genuine legal, factual, brand, privacy, or editorial restrictions.

help.why: The model cannot infer private restrictions, approved claims, or applicable jurisdiction.
help.example: List prohibited claims, required wording, jurisdiction, confidentiality limits, or topics to avoid.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.

variables.label: Additional context
variables.placeholder: Add any final context that materially affects the thread.
variables.hint: Do not repeat information already entered in another field.

help.why: One controlled catch-all field supports uncommon but material requirements without expanding the form.
help.example: Add any final context that materially affects the thread.
help.avoid: Invented facts, engagement bait, fake urgency, or undisclosed promotion.
